sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: RTX Corporation (NYSE: RTX) Looks Attractive After 13 Percent Three-Month Decline Even as Backlog Growth Signals Long-Term Strength
feed.summary: RTX Corporation has seen a 13.41% decline over the past three months, creating an attractive valuation despite strong fundamentals. Analysts hold a "Buy" consensus with significant upside potential, and the company's Q1 2026 results showed growth across all segments, driven by commercial aviation recovery and increased defense spending due to global conflicts. While GTF engine remediation remains a risk, management confidence in the outlook is high, supported by free cash flow guidance, backlog composition, and solid dividend payments.

feed.title: Aeva Technologies Projects Revenue Growth to 36 Million Dollars
feed.summary: Aeva Technologies Inc. is projecting a significant revenue increase of 70% to 100% for fiscal 2026, aiming for $30 million to $36 million, driven by growth in the lidar sensor market, defense spending, and commercial deployments. The company more than doubled its annual revenue to $18.1 million in 2025 and narrowed its adjusted losses. Oppenheimer has assigned an Outperform rating to AEVA, citing its unique Frequency Modulated Continuous Wave (FMCW) technology and strong financial position.

feed.title: Frank stuff: Costco's $1.50 hot dog deal might be unmatched in all of retail (COST:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: Costco recently made a minor change to its iconic $1.50 hot dog combo, now offering bottled water instead of soda. Despite the small profit margin, this deal serves as a significant loss leader, driving foot traffic and membership renewals. This strategy helps Costco maintain strong customer loyalty and brand equity, distinguishing it from most other retailers.

feed.title: Great Elm Capital Corp. ('GECC”) Schedules First Quarter 2026 Earnings Release and Conference Call

A conference call to discuss these results will be held on Tuesday, May 5, 2026, at 8:30 a.m. ET. Investors can access the call via dial-in numbers or a webcast available on the company's website.

feed.title: Farmland Shareholders Reaffirm Governance, Executive Pay Practices
feed.summary: Farmland Partners Inc. shareholders have reaffirmed the company's governance and executive pay practices during their annual meeting on April 28, 2026. They re-elected all five director nominees, ratified Crowe LLP as the independent auditor, and approved executive compensation on an advisory basis, opting for annual advisory votes. The company's stock, FPI, currently holds a "Hold" rating with a $12.50 price target.

feed.title: FSEP ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns BOATS:FSEP
feed.summary: This article provides an analysis of the FT Vest U.S. Equity Buffer ETF - September (FSEP), focusing on its dividends, returns, and operational details. FSEP uses options on the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) to buffer against the first 10% of losses over a one-year period, in exchange for foregoing some upside and SPY's dividends. Key information includes its $1.25 billion AUM, a 0.85% expense ratio, and its active management style, with the ETF not paying dividends directly to its holders.

feed.title: Investors Buy the Dip: Fidelity’s FSOL ETF Sees Big Inflow Despite Solana Slump
authors: TipRanks FX Crypto ETFs Auto-Generated Newsdesk
feed.summary: Despite a significant slump in Solana's price, Fidelity's Solana Fund (FSOL) ETF saw a substantial inflow of $7.79 million on April 30, 2026. This influx, representing 7.48% of the fund's assets under management, suggests that investors are viewing Solana's weakness as a buying opportunity. The move highlights the common investment strategy in digital assets of capitalizing on volatility, although it also increases potential downside risk if Solana's price continues to fall.
